finding: Interest rate tightening for Slovak firms sharply accelerated in wave 39
bullets: A net 43.8% of Slovak firms (n=76) reported tighter bank loan interest rates, rising sharply from a net 5.5% (n=62) in the prior quarter — a 38.3 pp reversal, implying significant upward pressure on borrowing costs.
Available loan or credit line size tightened for a net 12.4% of Slovak firms (n=74), up from a net 6.1% in wave 38, exceeding the EA's net 9.1%, signalling reduced access to financing.
Non-interest financing costs were unreportable for Slovakia in wave 39, while the EA measure eased by 6.9 pp to a net 29.7% (n=1,894) — leaving Slovak firms' cost structure opaque.
chart_subtitle: Net 43.8% of Slovak firms reported interest rate tightening
